title: Advertise in ChatGPT
author: montecarl
relevanceReason: 命中 3 个 AI/科技关键词。

comments.author: freediver
comments.textRawHtml: 7 years ago we started the 'you are not the product' movement for digital information consumption with Kagi Search. Since then the paradigm shifted from search to agents. Good to see the need for 'you are not the product' still holds up, maybe even more so now: an agent is only worth trusting if it works for you and nobody else. The second an advertiser gets between you and the answer, that's gone. So we're staying on the user-funded side of this. Boring, but it's the whole point.
comments.textZhHtml: 7 年前，我们通过 Kagi Search 发起了数字信息消费的“你不是产品”运动。从那时起，范式从搜索转向代理。很高兴看到“你不是产品”的需求仍然存在，也许现在更是如此：只有当代理对你而不是其他人有效时才值得信任。一旦广告商介入你和答案之间，一切就都消失了。因此，我们将继续坚持用户资助的方向。无聊，但这就是重点。

children.author: bayindirh
children.textRawHtml:
Kagi is not only an ad-free subscription. It’s a mental budget booster. Knowing there are no ads and results are higher quality removes a mental load I didn’t know I was carrying.

It’s possibly the single most impactful thing in my digital life.

Thanks for everything, and please keep doing what you’re doing.
children.textZhHtml:
Kagi 不仅是无广告订阅。这是一种精神预算助推器。知道没有广告并且结果质量更高，消除了我不知道自己承受的精神负担。

这可能是我的数字生活中最有影响力的事情。

感谢所做的一切，请继续做你正在做的事情。

children.author: phyalow
children.textRawHtml: These comments feel like llm authored astro turfing…
children.textZhHtml: 这些评论感觉像是 llm 创作的 astro turfing…

children.author: MarleTangible
children.textRawHtml: You know who else sounds like LLM from time to time? ESL - English as a Second Language. People learn English from books, and get introduced to phrases through reading or interacting with others within the same community. So, it sounds like you're using the LLM argument as a way to diminish their voices as well.
children.textZhHtml: 你知道还有谁听起来像 LLM 吗？ ESL——英语作为第二语言。人们从书本上学习英语，并通过阅读或与同一社区内的其他人互动来了解短语。所以，听起来你也在利用法学硕士的论点来削弱他们的声音。

children.author: ajmurmann
children.textRawHtml: As an ESL person who has spoken English as their main language for two decades now I find this very interesting. The vast majority of my English has been spoken with my wife who used to be a marketing VP or in a professional context. Sometimes people complain about the ways politicians or corporate executives talk and I don't get it and to me it sounds totally normal because that's what I'm used to. Makes you wonder how ESL speakers will sound more and more like LLMs because that's a large part of their exposure.
children.textZhHtml: 作为一名以英语为主要语言已有二十年的 ESL 人士，我发现这非常有趣。我的大部分英语都是和我的妻子说的，她曾经是营销副总裁或在专业环境中。有时人们抱怨政客或企业高管的谈话方式，但我不明白，对我来说这听起来完全正常，因为这就是我习惯的。让你想知道 ESL 演讲者如何听起来越来越像法学硕士，因为这是他们曝光的很大一部分。

children.author: radlad
children.textRawHtml: As a native English speaker, I worry about picking up LLM-speak simply due to interacting with Claude Code all day.
children.textZhHtml: 作为一名以英语为母语的人，我担心因为整天与 Claude Code 互动而无法学习 LLM 语言。

children.author: CGMthrowaway
children.textRawHtml: That's because many (most?) LLM chatbots were pre-trained by Kenyan ESL people
children.textZhHtml: 这是因为许多（大多数？）LLM 聊天机器人都是由肯尼亚 ESL 人员预先训练的

children.author: neonstatic
children.textRawHtml: As an ESL, it hurts me to see the atrophy of written English. "Your right!" is just painful.
children.textZhHtml: 作为一名 ESL 学生，看到书面英语的萎缩让我很伤心。 “你的权利！”只是痛苦。

children.author: lcnPylGDnU4H9OF
children.textRawHtml: As an English phrase, that isn't strictly wrong, but I understand what you mean and it's your right to complain. ;)
children.textZhHtml:
作为一个英语短语，严格来说这并没有错，但我明白你的意思，你有权利抱怨。 ;)
我认为我们很好https://www.instagram.com/ahh.gpt/reel/Da12ufvt69B/

children.author: InsideOutSanta
children.textRawHtml:
I'm a customer of Kagi who is in no way affiliated with Kagi, other than sending them money. I agree with bayindirh: It’s possibly the single most impactful thing in my digital life.

I know that we live in a world where tech increasingly sucks, and positive comments on tech are (correctly!) seen as suspicious. The thing, though, is that Kagi is genuinely just a great product, and search is such a central part of using the Internet that good, trustworthy search makes a huge difference.
children.textZhHtml:
我是 Kagi 的客户，除了向 Kagi 汇款外，与 Kagi 没有任何关系。我同意 bayindirh 的观点：这可能是我的数字生活中最有影响力的事情。

我知道我们生活在一个技术越来越糟糕的世界，对技术的积极评论（正确地！）被视为可疑。但问题是，Kagi 确实是一款出色的产品，而搜索是使用互联网的核心部分，良好、值得信赖的搜索会产生巨大的影响。

children.author: bilbo0s
children.textRawHtml:
>trustworthy search makes a huge difference

That's the rub. Today on the internet, no one can validate that there exists any "trustworthy search". If we don't control the servers and validate the software on it, then we are taking someone's word for it. Most often that someone is a marketing person at the hosting organization. Occasionally it's a random comment on the internet behind a pseudonym we know nothing about.

People are becoming more and more, justifiably, cynical. And it's good for society that they are.
children.textZhHtml:
>值得信赖的搜索会产生巨大的差异

这就是问题所在。今天在互联网上，没有人可以验证是否存在任何“值得信赖的搜索”。如果我们不控制服务器并验证服务器上的软件，那么我们就会相信某人的话。最常见的是，某人是托管组织的营销人员。有时，这是互联网上以我们一无所知的笔名背后的随机评论。

人们变得越来越愤世嫉俗，这是理所当然的。它们对社会有好处。

children.author: InsideOutSanta
children.textRawHtml:
> If we don't control the servers and validate the software on it, then we are taking someone's word for it

That's what trust is, though. I find Kagi worthy of trust since I have no evidence that they are doing anything that would violate my trust in them, and their incentives align with mine. I give them money, they give me good search results.

> People are becoming more and more, justifiably, cynical. And it's good for society that they are.

I do not believe that this is good. Democracies are built on trust. Without trust, they die, as we can plainly see in the US right now, where people are so distrustful of each other that it is starting to destroy the very fabric holding together society itself.
children.textZhHtml:
>如果我们不控制服务器并验证其上的软件，那么我们就会相信某人的话

但这就是信任。我发现卡吉值得信任，因为我没有证据表明他们正在做任何违反我对他们的信任的事情，而且他们的动机与我的一致。我给他们钱，他们给我很好的搜索结果。

>人们变得越来越愤世嫉俗，这是理所当然的。他们这样对社会有好处。

我不认为这是好事。民主是建立在信任之上的。没有信任，他们就会死亡，正如我们现在在美国可以清楚地看到的那样，人们彼此如此不信任，以至于开始破坏维系社会本身的结构。

children.author: bilbo0s
children.textRawHtml:
The entire point of the Constitution was to not trust each other. We wouldn't even need checks or balances if trust were a reasonable governing philosophy. Nor a Bill of Rights, nor any explicit admonition against slavery.

The reason we need these and many more is that we don't trust each other. And it's wise that we don't.

This doesn't mean trust is bad, only that the founders, rightfully, knew that it leads to complacency.

What's landed us where we are is not a lack of trust. (We've lacked trust from day one, that's how we got the Constitution in the first place). What's landed us here is complacency.
children.textZhHtml:
宪法的全部要点是不相互信任。如果信任是一种合理的治理理念，我们甚至不需要制衡。也没有权利法案，也没有任何反对奴隶制的明确警告。

我们需要这些以及更多的原因是我们彼此不信任。我们不这样做是明智的。

这并不意味着信任不好，只是创始人理所当然地知道信任会导致自满。

我们之所以走到今天这一步，并不是因为缺乏信任。 （我们从第一天起就缺乏信任，这就是我们最初制定宪法的方式）。让我们来到这里的是自满。

children.author: InsideOutSanta
children.textRawHtml: It's distrust - in foreigners, in each other - that got extremists elected, and it's distrust - in the media, in the institutions - that gives them unchecked power to destroy the country.
children.textZhHtml: 正是对外国人、彼此之间的不信任让极端分子当选，正是对媒体、机构的不信任赋予了他们摧毁国家的不受限制的权力。

children.author: bilbo0s
children.textRawHtml:
None of which would have happened if we had not gotten so complacent about defending our democracy through vigorous enforcement of the Constitution, and voting in a fashion more informed by wisdom than emotion.

At the root of all you lament is not lack of trust, it is complacency. Decades long complacency.

Lack of trust is what our republic was founded on, and the entire reason it was structured the way it was. Our republic is specifically designed to function in a trust-free environment.

But no republic, nor any other organizational construct, no matter how well designed, will survive complacency.
children.textZhHtml:
如果我们没有如此自满地通过大力执行宪法来捍卫我们的民主，并以智慧而非情感的方式进行投票，那么这一切都不会发生。

您所哀叹的根源不是缺乏信任，而是自满。几十年来的自满情绪。

缺乏信任是我们共和国的基础，也是它如此构建的全部原因。我们的共和国是专门设计在无信任环境中运作的。

但是，任何共和国或任何其他组织结构，无论设计得多么好，都无法自满。
